signals.idea: Add adjustment layer / effect layer clips to the timeline. Users can drag a continuous effect from the effect creator onto a visual track, creating an 'effect clip' that applies its effect to all clips on tracks below it for its duration. Key concepts: (1) New clip type 'effect-layer' — a clip on a visual track that has no media content, just an effect applied to everything beneath it. (2) Can be created by dragging from the effect creator panel or from a new 'Add Effect Layer' button. (3) The effect layer clip is resizable and movable like any other clip. (4) Rendering: the Remotion composition needs to identify effect-layer clips and wrap all lower-track content within that time range with the effect component. (5) Effect layers show in the timeline with a distinctive visual style (e.g. semi-transparent, different color) so users can tell them apart from media clips. (6) Effect layers can use any continuous effect (built-in or custom) with tuneable parameters. (7) Multiple effect layers can stack — they compose in track order (top affects everything below it).

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect drag UX: Step 4 targets the wrong surface for drag creation. In this repo, the named `EffectCreatorPanel` is a modal effect editor, not a persistent palette users can drag from, so that step does not actually implement the requested drag-from-effect-creator workflow.

Preview overlay editing: The plan misses the preview overlay path. If an effect-layer clip can be selected, the current overlay editor will treat it like a visual clip and show a full-frame move/crop box, allowing invalid geometry edits on a clip that should have no spatial bounds.

Timeline compositing order: The plan claims overlapping effect layers on the same track should work, but the current preview renderer has no deterministic within-track z-order beyond start time. Without carrying clip order into rendering or forbidding same-track overlap, stacked same-track effect layers will compose incidentally rather than by a defined rule.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 6.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items: New `'effect-layer'` ClipType is recognized throughout the system without errors
Effect-layer clips can be created via UI (button or drag) and placed on visual tracks
Effect-layer clips render correctly in Remotion preview — the continuous effect visually applies to all clips on tracks below the effect layer for its duration
Multiple stacked effect layers compose correctly (top layer's effect wraps everything below including other effect layers' output)
Effect-layer clips have distinctive visual appearance in the timeline (different icon, semi-transparent/colored background)
Properties panel shows only relevant controls for effect-layer clips (continuous effect selector, intensity, params, timing)
Effect-layer clips can be resized, moved, and split like other clips
Save → reload round-trip preserves effect-layer clips with all their settings
Effect-layer clips on audio tracks are rejected/prevented
Partial time overlaps work correctly — the effect only applies during the effect-layer clip's duration, not before or after

Step 9 only narrows tabs in `ClipPanel`; it does not cover this editor.

unresolved_flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:58-72,146-175` maintains `clipOrder` in editor state, but `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx:9-10,18,27-32,81-96` groups clips only from `config.clips` and sorts them only by `at`.
